methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mariana Minerals is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Turner Caldwell that has raised $95M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 private peers at identical stage and sector (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, Steady Energy at $110M) plus 1 public comparable and additional private reference points including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, using total capital raised as a proxy base, applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $190M. The peer-median method across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables produced a $150M indication, with the peer range spanning $110M to $200M. These approaches triangulate to a median implied valuation of $170M, with a low bound of $128M and high bound of $218M.

The $170M median valuation sits midway between the two methods and aligns with the peer median. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 3-peer sample for Mariana's specific technology maturity and market position, and whether the 2.0x stage multiplier accurately reflects investor expectations for this subsector. The $128M–$218M range reflects these methodological bounds and peer dispersion.
